popupRules: Do not open detail popup for undiscovered entries.
Do not show zone/location inside popup because the zone is already selected in Fishdex.
Show howToCatch only when it is not null.
Mastery/current bonus should be computed dynamically from save data and zone mastery rules; this file provides static encyclopedia text and catch conditions.

crucian_carp.name: Crucian Carp
crucian_carp.description: A hardy pond fish that thrives almost anywhere and patiently waits for careless anglers.

roach.name: Roach
roach.description: A small silver freshwater fish that bites often and helps new anglers learn the basics.

small_perch.name: Small Perch
small_perch.description: A striped little hunter with more attitude than size.

stickleback.name: Stickleback
stickleback.description: Tiny, spiky and surprisingly brave for something barely heavier than a coin.

common_bream.name: Common Bream
common_bream.description: A broad-bodied bottom feeder often found cruising slowly through muddy water.

tench.name: Tench
tench.description: A greenish, slippery fish that loves calm water and thick vegetation.

goldfish.name: Goldfish
goldfish.description: Probably released from someone's aquarium. It seems to be doing suspiciously well.

stone_loach.name: Stone Loach
stone_loach.description: A bottom-dwelling fish that hides between stones and pretends not to exist.

pumpkinseed.name: Pumpkinseed
pumpkinseed.description: A colorful little sunfish that looks far too tropical for this pond.

weatherfish.name: Weatherfish
weatherfish.description: A strange eel-like fish said to become restless before the weather changes.

giant_crucian_carp.name: Giant Crucian Carp
giant_crucian_carp.description: An unusually large crucian carp with the confidence of a lake monster.

morning_perch.name: Morning Perch
morning_perch.description: A perch that seems most active when the morning light hits the water.

afternoon_roach.name: Afternoon Roach
afternoon_roach.description: A relaxed midday roach that shows up when the pond is at its warmest.

reed.name: Reed
reed.description: Tall shoreline stems that provide cover for small fish and noisy insects.

lily_pad.name: Lily Pad
lily_pad.description: A floating leaf platform, perfect for frogs and dramatic pond reflections.

algae_clump.name: Algae Clump
algae_clump.description: A soft green mass that proves the pond is alive, even if your hook disagrees.

water_hyacinth.name: Water Hyacinth
water_hyacinth.description: A floating plant with pretty flowers and a talent for spreading everywhere.

hornwort.name: Hornwort
hornwort.description: A submerged plant that gives tiny fish a place to hide.

duckweed.name: Duckweed
duckweed.description: Tiny floating leaves that can cover a pond faster than expected.

cattail.name: Cattail
cattail.description: A marsh plant with brown spikes that look like nature's corn dogs.

watercress.name: Watercress
watercress.description: A peppery aquatic plant that prefers cool, moving water.

arrowhead_plant.name: Arrowhead Plant
arrowhead_plant.description: A wetland plant named for its arrow-shaped leaves.

water_mint.name: Water Mint
water_mint.description: A fragrant plant that makes the shoreline smell fresher than the trash suggests.

old_boot.name: Old Boot
old_boot.description: Someone lost a boot and apparently decided walking home with one shoe was acceptable.

tin_can.name: Tin Can
tin_can.description: A tiny monument to bad aim and worse recycling habits.

plastic_bag.name: Plastic Bag
plastic_bag.description: It went out for groceries and came back as pond decoration.

glass_bottle.name: Glass Bottle
glass_bottle.description: Message inside: please stop throwing bottles into the water.

rusty_hook.name: Rusty Hook
rusty_hook.description: A retired hook still trying to be useful, mostly by being sharp.

broken_rod.name: Broken Rod
broken_rod.description: The remains of an angler who blamed the equipment, probably correctly.

old_tire.name: Old Tire
old_tire.description: Nobody knows how a tire found the pond, but it seems very committed to staying.

rubber_duck.name: Rubber Duck
rubber_duck.description: A bath-time veteran now bravely exploring open water.

garden_glove.name: Garden Glove
garden_glove.description: One glove went gardening. The other chose a life at sea.

bicycle_wheel.name: Bicycle Wheel
bicycle_wheel.description: Proof that someone attempted a shortcut and the pond won.

chub.name: Chub
chub.description: A bold river fish that is rarely picky and often first to the bait.

grayling.name: Grayling
grayling.description: A graceful river fish with a sail-like fin and a taste for clear, fast water.

barbel.name: Barbel
barbel.description: A strong bottom feeder built for currents and stubborn fights.

brown_trout.name: Brown Trout
brown_trout.description: A cautious predator of clean water, famous for making anglers feel underprepared.

pike.name: Pike
pike.description: An ambush predator with sharp teeth and very little patience.

burbot.name: Burbot
burbot.description: A cold-water oddball that looks like a catfish had an argument with an eel.

dawn_trout.name: Dawn Trout
dawn_trout.description: A trout that appears with the first pale light of morning.

midnight_eel.name: Midnight Eel
midnight_eel.description: A slippery night wanderer that prefers the river when everyone else is asleep.

water_crowfoot.name: Water Crowfoot
water_crowfoot.description: A delicate river plant with white flowers and finely divided underwater leaves.

river_weed.name: River Weed
river_weed.description: A stubborn green tangle that bends with the current but rarely lets go.

submerged_moss.name: Submerged Moss
submerged_moss.description: Soft underwater growth clinging to stones in the current.

water_celery.name: Water Celery
water_celery.description: A leafy aquatic plant that sounds more edible than it probably is.

flowering_rush.name: Flowering Rush
flowering_rush.description: A waterside plant with pink blooms and a surprisingly elegant name.

mare_s_tail.name: Mare's Tail
mare_s_tail.description: A neat aquatic plant with upright shoots that resemble tiny green bottle brushes.

fishing_net_fragment.name: Fishing Net Fragment
fishing_net_fragment.description: A torn piece of net that failed at catching fish but succeeded at catching your hook.

plastic_bottle.name: Plastic Bottle
plastic_bottle.description: Still floating proudly, as if buoyancy were an achievement.

car_part.name: Car Part
car_part.description: Somewhere, a mechanic is missing a clue.

rope.name: Rope
rope.description: A length of rope with no boat, no knot and no explanation.

broken_glass_jar.name: Broken Glass Jar
broken_glass_jar.description: Once held jam. Now holds disappointment.

shopping_bag.name: Shopping Bag
shopping_bag.description: It drifted downstream with the confidence of a tiny sailboat.

large_perch.name: Large Perch
large_perch.description: A bigger, bolder perch that patrols the lake like it owns the dock.

carp.name: Carp
carp.description: A heavy, cautious fish that prefers mud, patience and questionable snacks.

zander.name: Zander
zander.description: A sharp-eyed predator often active in dim light and deeper water.

whitefish.name: Whitefish
whitefish.description: A clean, silvery fish that prefers cooler open water.

vendace.name: Vendace
vendace.description: A small schooling fish that makes up for size with numbers.

eel.name: Eel
eel.description: A mysterious, snake-like fish that always looks like it knows a secret route.

european_catfish.name: European Catfish
european_catfish.description: A huge freshwater predator with whiskers, patience and a vacuum-cleaner mouth.

evening_catfish.name: Evening Catfish
evening_catfish.description: A catfish that begins roaming as the lake grows quiet in the evening.

night_pike.name: Night Pike
night_pike.description: A pike that hunts in darkness, because daylight apparently was too easy.

pre_dawn_zander.name: Pre-dawn Zander
pre_dawn_zander.description: A zander that bites just before sunrise, when only patient anglers are awake.

yellow_water_lily.name: Yellow Water Lily
yellow_water_lily.description: A bright floating lily that brings a little sunshine to still water.

white_water_lily.name: White Water Lily
white_water_lily.description: A classic lake flower with calm leaves and elegant white petals.

quillwort.name: Quillwort
quillwort.description: A small, ancient-looking aquatic plant that keeps to the lakebed.

water_soldier.name: Water Soldier
water_soldier.description: A spiky floating plant with a name far more dramatic than its lifestyle.

bladderwort.name: Bladderwort
bladderwort.description: A carnivorous aquatic plant that traps tiny prey underwater.

water_violet.name: Water Violet
water_violet.description: A delicate flowering plant found in quiet, shallow waters.

bulrush.name: Bulrush
bulrush.description: Tall reeds that mark shallow water and excellent hiding places.

anchor.name: Anchor
anchor.description: A small anchor that clearly gave up on holding anything in place.

sunken_buoy.name: Sunken Buoy
sunken_buoy.description: A buoy whose only job was floating. It had one job.

outboard_motor_part.name: Outboard Motor Part
outboard_motor_part.description: A broken engine piece from a boat that probably made an expensive noise first.

beer_cans.name: Beer Cans
beer_cans.description: Evidence of a lakeside party with terrible cleanup skills.

waterlogged_suitcase.name: Waterlogged Suitcase
waterlogged_suitcase.description: Packed for a trip and somehow took the lake route.

trolling_weight.name: Trolling Weight
trolling_weight.description: Heavy, metal and exactly where it should not be.

bicycle.name: Bicycle
bicycle.description: A full bicycle. The fish refuse to say who was riding it.

baltic_herring.name: Baltic Herring
baltic_herring.description: A small schooling fish that proves quantity has a quality of its own.

smelt.name: Smelt
smelt.description: A tiny silver fish with a surprisingly strong personality.

sprat.name: Sprat
sprat.description: Small, fast and almost impossible to take seriously until there are thousands of them.

flounder.name: Flounder
flounder.description: A flatfish that looks like it was designed by someone who ran out of symmetry.

mackerel.name: Mackerel
mackerel.description: A fast striped fish that moves in schools and rarely slows down for anyone.

garfish.name: Garfish
garfish.description: A long, needle-nosed fish that looks like it is always pointing somewhere important.

seabass.name: Seabass
seabass.description: A prized coastal predator with a clean silver shine and a strong fight.

sea_trout.name: Sea Trout
sea_trout.description: A powerful trout that trades freshwater habits for saltwater adventures.

bladderwrack.name: Bladderwrack
bladderwrack.description: A common brown seaweed with air bladders that help it float.

sea_lettuce.name: Sea Lettuce
sea_lettuce.description: A bright green seaweed that looks exactly like its name suggests.

kelp_frond.name: Kelp Frond
kelp_frond.description: A torn strip of kelp drifting where currents decide.

eelgrass.name: Eelgrass
eelgrass.description: A flowering marine plant that forms underwater meadows for small creatures.

coralline_algae.name: Coralline Algae
coralline_algae.description: Hard pinkish algae that gives rocks a coral-like coating.

irish_moss.name: Irish Moss
irish_moss.description: A red seaweed often found clinging to rocky shores.

dulse.name: Dulse
dulse.description: A reddish edible seaweed with a long history in coastal diets.

rockweed.name: Rockweed
rockweed.description: A tough brown seaweed built for waves, rocks and bad moods.

lobster_trap.name: Lobster Trap
lobster_trap.description: A trap that caught no lobster today, but proudly caught your line.

lost_anchor_chain.name: Lost Anchor Chain
lost_anchor_chain.description: Heavy iron links from something that once insisted it was secure.

old_crab_pot.name: Old Crab Pot
old_crab_pot.description: The crab left. The pot stayed. Everyone made choices.

oil_drum.name: Oil Drum
oil_drum.description: An industrial drum that rolled into the water and decided to become scenery.

lost_propeller.name: Lost Propeller
lost_propeller.description: A boat somewhere became much less boat-like after losing this.

diver_s_fin.name: Diver's Fin
diver_s_fin.description: One flipper from a diver who probably swam in circles afterward.

tangled_net.name: Tangled Net
tangled_net.description: A chaotic knot of rope that has declared war on hooks everywhere.

cod.name: Cod
cod.description: A classic northern fish with a big mouth and a long history on dinner plates.

haddock.name: Haddock
haddock.description: A clean-looking fish often found near the seafloor in cool waters.

redfish.name: Redfish
redfish.description: A deep-water fish with bright color and a patient, slow-growing life.

salmon.name: Salmon
salmon.description: A strong migratory fish famous for power, stamina and dramatic journeys.

halibut.name: Halibut
halibut.description: A massive flatfish that can make even experienced anglers reconsider their equipment.

wolffish.name: Wolffish
wolffish.description: A rugged fish with strong jaws and the expression of someone who bites rocks for fun.

giant_kelp.name: Giant Kelp
giant_kelp.description: A towering seaweed that can form underwater forests.

sugar_kelp.name: Sugar Kelp
sugar_kelp.description: A brown kelp with long blades and a name sweeter than its habitat.

bootlace_weed.name: Bootlace Weed
bootlace_weed.description: A stringy seaweed that looks like it escaped from an old shoe.

sea_oak.name: Sea Oak
sea_oak.description: A branching brown seaweed named for its oak-like shape.

oarweed.name: Oarweed
oarweed.description: A long kelp blade that looks ready to row away.

pepper_dulse.name: Pepper Dulse
pepper_dulse.description: A small red seaweed known for its spicy flavor.

sunken_chest.name: Sunken Chest
sunken_chest.description: A chest from somewhere important, containing mostly questions.

old_naval_mine.name: Old Naval Mine
old_naval_mine.description: Thankfully inactive. Probably. Best not to poke it.

ship_s_bell.name: Ship's Bell
ship_s_bell.description: A brass bell that once rang across a deck and now startles crabs.

old_lantern.name: Old Lantern
old_lantern.description: A weathered lantern that has not lit anything except your curiosity in years.

ship_wheel.name: Ship Wheel
ship_wheel.description: A steering wheel from a vessel that lost both direction and dignity.

naval_flag.name: Naval Flag
naval_flag.description: A faded flag that still looks proud despite being thoroughly soaked.

atlantic_mackerel.name: Atlantic Mackerel
atlantic_mackerel.description: A fast open-water fish with shimmering stripes and endless energy.

tuna.name: Tuna
tuna.description: A powerful ocean predator built like a missile with fins.

mahi_mahi.name: Mahi-Mahi
mahi_mahi.description: A colorful tropical fish that looks like the ocean gave it a paint job.

swordfish.name: Swordfish
swordfish.description: A sleek predator armed with a long bill and serious speed.

marlin.name: Marlin
marlin.description: A legendary sport fish known for huge leaps and exhausting fights.

oarfish.name: Oarfish
oarfish.description: A long, mysterious deep-water fish that looks like a sea serpent's polite cousin.

giant_squid.name: Giant Squid
giant_squid.description: A deep-ocean giant with enormous eyes and the patience to become a myth.

coelacanth.name: Coelacanth
coelacanth.description: A living fossil once thought extinct, still hiding in the dark like it missed the memo.

deep_sea_coral.name: Deep Sea Coral
deep_sea_coral.description: A slow-growing deep-sea structure that shelters life far from sunlight.

black_smoker_vent_algae.name: Black Smoker Vent Algae
black_smoker_vent_algae.description: Life clinging near volcanic vents where sunlight never reaches.

midnight_zone_kelp.name: Midnight Zone Kelp
midnight_zone_kelp.description: A strange deep-water kelp adapted to dim, cold conditions.

bioluminescent_algae.name: Bioluminescent Algae
bioluminescent_algae.description: Tiny glowing organisms that make darkness look alive.

abyssal_sea_grass.name: Abyssal Sea Grass
abyssal_sea_grass.description: A hardy deep-sea plant-like growth from the edge of the unknown.

giant_tube_worm_cluster.name: Giant Tube Worm Cluster
giant_tube_worm_cluster.description: A cluster of deep-sea worms thriving near chemical-rich vents.

sea_fan.name: Sea Fan
sea_fan.description: A delicate branching organism shaped like an underwater fan.

sunken_aircraft_part.name: Sunken Aircraft Part
sunken_aircraft_part.description: A piece of aircraft that took the phrase 'water landing' too seriously.

deep_sea_pressure_gauge.name: Deep Sea Pressure Gauge
deep_sea_pressure_gauge.description: It measured pressure until the ocean won the argument.

lost_submarine.name: Lost Submarine
lost_submarine.description: A whole submarine would be impressive. This is at least a concerning hint.

ancient_amphora.name: Ancient Amphora
ancient_amphora.description: An old ceramic vessel from a time when shipping insurance was probably worse.

black_box.name: Black Box
black_box.description: A mysterious recorder that knows exactly what happened and refuses to explain.

whale_bones.name: Whale Bones
whale_bones.description: Huge old bones resting quietly where giants once passed.

submarine_cable.name: Submarine Cable
submarine_cable.description: A thick cable that once carried signals and now carries seaweed.

catfish.name: European Catfish
catfish.description: A huge freshwater predator with whiskers, patience and a vacuum-cleaner mouth.

predawn_zander.name: Pre-dawn Zander
predawn_zander.description: A zander that bites just before sunrise, when only patient anglers are awake.

net_fragment.name: Fishing Net Fragment
net_fragment.description: A torn piece of net that failed at catching fish but succeeded at catching your hook.

broken_jar.name: Broken Glass Jar
broken_jar.description: Once held jam. Now holds disappointment.

motor_part.name: Outboard Motor Part
motor_part.description: A broken engine piece from a boat that probably made an expensive noise first.

wet_suitcase.name: Waterlogged Suitcase
wet_suitcase.description: Packed for a trip and somehow took the lake route.

troll_weight.name: Trolling Weight
troll_weight.description: Heavy, metal and exactly where it should not be.

sunken_bicycle.name: Bicycle
sunken_bicycle.description: A full bicycle. The fish refuse to say who was riding it.

anchor_chain.name: Lost Anchor Chain
anchor_chain.description: Heavy iron links from something that once insisted it was secure.

crab_pot.name: Old Crab Pot
crab_pot.description: The crab left. The pot stayed. Everyone made choices.

propeller.name: Lost Propeller
propeller.description: A boat somewhere became much less boat-like after losing this.

divers_fin.name: Diver's Fin
divers_fin.description: One flipper from a diver who probably swam in circles afterward.

naval_mine.name: Old Naval Mine
naval_mine.description: Thankfully inactive. Probably. Best not to poke it.

ships_bell.name: Ship's Bell
ships_bell.description: A brass bell that once rang across a deck and now startles crabs.

aircraft_part.name: Sunken Aircraft Part
aircraft_part.description: A piece of aircraft that took the phrase 'water landing' too seriously.

pressure_gauge.name: Deep Sea Pressure Gauge
pressure_gauge.description: It measured pressure until the ocean won the argument.

amphora.name: Ancient Amphora
amphora.description: An old ceramic vessel from a time when shipping insurance was probably worse.

sub_cable.name: Submarine Cable
sub_cable.description: A thick cable that once carried signals and now carries seaweed.

mares_tail.name: Mare's Tail
mares_tail.description: A neat aquatic plant with upright shoots that resemble tiny green bottle brushes.

yellow_lily.name: Yellow Water Lily
yellow_lily.description: A bright floating lily that brings a little sunshine to still water.

white_lily.name: White Water Lily
white_lily.description: A classic lake flower with calm leaves and elegant white petals.

deep_coral.name: Deep Sea Coral
deep_coral.description: A slow-growing deep-sea structure that shelters life far from sunlight.

vent_algae.name: Black Smoker Vent Algae
vent_algae.description: Life clinging near volcanic vents where sunlight never reaches.

midnight_kelp.name: Midnight Zone Kelp
midnight_kelp.description: A strange deep-water kelp adapted to dim, cold conditions.

biolum_algae.name: Bioluminescent Algae
biolum_algae.description: Tiny glowing organisms that make darkness look alive.

abyssal_grass.name: Abyssal Sea Grass
abyssal_grass.description: A hardy deep-sea plant-like growth from the edge of the unknown.

tube_worm.name: Giant Tube Worm Cluster
tube_worm.description: A cluster of deep-sea worms thriving near chemical-rich vents.

crimson_crown_perch.name: Crimson Crown Perch
crimson_crown_perch.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
crimson_crown_perch.description: A pond perch with brilliant crimson dorsal fins that form a regal crown-like crest. Ancient anglers believed this fish granted courage to whoever landed it. Found only in ponds undisturbed for a century or more, where the water runs too still for ordinary fishermen to bother casting.

golden_veil_carp.name: Golden Veil Carp
golden_veil_carp.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
golden_veil_carp.description: A carp whose scales shift between deep gold and amber like sunlight filtering through still water. Said to appear only when the pond is perfectly calm and the angler has waited long enough to forget why they came. Local legend holds that its presence turns the water the colour of summer afternoons.

silver_ribbon_loach.name: Silver Ribbon Loach
silver_ribbon_loach.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
silver_ribbon_loach.description: A loach with a continuous silver stripe along its flank that shimmers like a ribbon of moonlight on moving water. Known to surface only before storms and on nights when the sky holds no stars. Fishermen who glimpse it report an inexplicable sense of calm that lasts for days.

emerald_grayling.name: Emerald Grayling
emerald_grayling.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
emerald_grayling.description: A grayling with emerald-tinted scales that catch fast current like gemstones tumbling in a jeweller's tray. Extremely rare; once considered sacred by riverside cultures who believed the fish carried the river's memory within its scales. No two emerald graylings bear the same exact pattern.

marbleback_barbel.name: Marbleback Barbel
marbleback_barbel.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
marbleback_barbel.description: A barbel whose back is patterned with swirling marble-like markings unique to each individual. Found only in fast-flowing stretches where few anglers dare to wade, clinging to the riverbed with extraordinary strength. River guides speak of it in the same reverent tones as they do white-water crossings.

redfin_chub.name: Redfin Chub
redfin_chub.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
redfin_chub.description: A chub with blazing crimson fins said to be touched by ancient river spirits. Its fins glow faintly in dim light near dusk, and fishermen who have caught one describe the fins as warm to the touch despite the cold river water. The colour fades completely within minutes of landing.

blueglass_char.name: Blueglass Char
blueglass_char.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
blueglass_char.description: A char with near-translucent blue-tinted scales that reveal shadowy traces of its inner structure in bright sunlight. Found only in the deepest, coldest lake waters where temperatures hover just above freezing year-round. Scientists debate whether its transparency is a predation defence or a quirk of isolation.

copperplate_bream.name: Copperplate Bream
copperplate_bream.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
copperplate_bream.description: A bream with scales burnished like hammered copper plate, each one catching light at a slightly different angle. Its extraordinary pigmentation results from centuries of isolation in mineral-rich lake sediment. Under direct sunlight the flanks appear almost molten, a sight that has stopped many an angler mid-cast.

frostback_pike.name: Frostback Pike
frostback_pike.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
frostback_pike.description: A pike with a silver-white back frosted with pale blue markings that look like cracks in winter ice. Said to hibernate beneath actual lake ice for years at a time, emerging only in the deepest cold spells when other pike have retreated to warmer depths. Catching one is considered an omen of an unusually hard winter.

copperbelly_mullet.name: Copperbelly Mullet
copperbelly_mullet.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
copperbelly_mullet.description: A mullet with a copper-hued underbelly that catches moonlight with an almost metallic gleam. Said to navigate bay currents by detecting subtle magnetic variations invisible to every other species. Harbour workers who spot one in the shallows at night claim the water around it ripples outward in perfect concentric rings.

golden_flounder.name: Golden Flounder
golden_flounder.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
golden_flounder.description: A flounder of extraordinary all-over golden colouration, reported once per generation in any given bay. Fishermen who catch one often keep it secret for years, fearing rivals will stake out the same waters. Both eyes are positioned above the midline, giving it an unsettlingly direct upward gaze from the seabed.

silver_needlefish.name: Silver Needlefish
silver_needlefish.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
silver_needlefish.description: A needlefish with scales so uniformly silver they resemble polished steel in direct light. Moves with such velocity through shallow bay waters that it registers as little more than a horizontal flash. Anglers who have landed one describe the moment of capture as sudden silence followed by a weight that felt entirely at odds with the fish's size.

arctic_wolffish.name: Arctic Wolffish
arctic_wolffish.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
arctic_wolffish.description: A wolffish from the northernmost Arctic waters, bearing pale blue lateral stripes not found in any common population. Its teeth can crush sea urchins and thick-shelled molluscs with a force that sounds like cracking stone. Fishermen who haul one up describe the eyes as the colour of pack ice — blue-grey and utterly still.

crimson_rockfish.name: Crimson Rockfish
crimson_rockfish.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
crimson_rockfish.description: A deep-sea rockfish of vivid uniform crimson, dwelling in rock crevices where daylight barely reaches. Its bright colour is thought to confuse predators in the red-filtered light of deep water, where crimson reads as black. Individual fish have been aged at over two hundred years by growth-ring analysis.

stormscale_cod.name: Stormscale Cod
stormscale_cod.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
stormscale_cod.description: A cod whose dark-edged scales form a swirling pattern that mirrors storm-cloud formations. Ancient sailors believed catching one foretold clear weather ahead, interpreting it as the sea releasing a storm it no longer needed. Its flesh is said to taste faintly of salt air even after cooking — a claim that cannot be verified because no one who catches one will eat it.

obsidian_moonfish.name: Obsidian Moonfish
obsidian_moonfish.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
obsidian_moonfish.description: A moonfish of deep obsidian black with iridescent edges that catch no light until they catch all of it at once. Believed to be a navigator species guiding other fish migrations across featureless ocean. Observed only during the darkest phases of new moon nights in open water far from any shore, appearing as though assembled from the darkness itself.

sapphire_spearfish.name: Sapphire Spearfish
sapphire_spearfish.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
sapphire_spearfish.description: A spearfish of extraordinary deep-ocean sapphire blue, found in conditions so extreme that the pressure alone would crush ordinary bone. Its elongated bill is denser than any metal the ocean has offered up, and the colour does not fade in air the way that deep-sea colouration usually does — it intensifies instead, becoming richer the longer the fish is above water.

suncrest_mahi.name: Suncrest Mahi
suncrest_mahi.howToCatch: Ultra Rare — 1 in 50,000,000 chance. Cannot be targeted.
suncrest_mahi.description: A mahi-mahi with a golden solar crest that blazes like concentrated sunlight on open water. Known to leap higher than any other mahi-mahi population and to spin at the apex of each leap in a way that scatters light across the surface in all directions. Anglers who have fought one describe the fight as thirty minutes of losing, followed by a single inexplicable moment of winning.
